title: An ancient boundary stone with one deeply carved groove. Precept's governing rules are like marks inscribed in stone: applied once at compile time, impossible to erase or fill at runtime. The groove is the precept — the single structural rule that holds permanently in the material. The stone fragment is not neat or digital; it is material, permanent, and physically prior to any execution.
conceptualBridge: The incised stone means Precept's rules are permanently carved into the definition because compile-time verification is the act of inscription — the rule is in the material before any runtime exists, and no execution can alter it.
decision: Rejected: 3.75 avg below threshold; legibility hard fail (3 < 3 minimum) — the irregular stone shard and carved groove are not readable at 32px, and the visual economy is too low. The concept is valid but this execution does not pass.
